cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Sean could have gone to the bakery. First, the question says the bakery was closed after 9pm. So the latest he could have gone would be before or at 9pm. Let's check all the times Sean was seen doing other things. Sean woke up at 5am. Then: - From 5am to 6am, he was in class with James. - From 6am to 8am, David saw him at the deli for lunch. - From 8am to 9am, Betty saw him at the mall buying clothes. - From 9am to 5pm, Linda saw him on a rooftop. - From 8pm to 9pm, Tiffany saw him driving to the water park. So we need to look for any gaps between these times when Sean wasn't accounted for, and the bakery is still open (before 9pm). Let me list out the timeline: 1. 5am to 6am: class 2. 6am to 8am: deli 3. 8am to 9am: mall 4. 9am to 5pm: rooftop 5. 5pm to 8pm: not accounted for yet 6. 8pm to 9pm: driving to water park 7. 9pm onwards: bakery closed So the only time Sean isn't doing anything is between 5pm and 8pm. The bakery is open during that time (since it's closed only after 9pm). But let me double check the options given: (A) 8pm to 9pm - During this time, he was seen at the water park (B) 5pm to 8pm - This is when he wasn't seen doing anything, and the bakery
